title: Feature Report — Women's Viperball League (WVL): Galactic Premiership
scope: A complete 64-team, 4-tier women's professional league with EPL-style promotion/relegation, operating as a standalone game mode where the human plays as a club owner (not a coach).

rationale.item: College women's players in the CVL (187 teams across 13 conferences) graduate into a dead end. Their only post-college path is FIV international play — there's no women's professional league.
College dynasty mode builds attachment to players across 4 years, and then those players have nowhere to go.

delegation_events.decision: Owner Mode design philosophy: ownership sim, not coaching sim — high-level decisions, layers of delegation
delegation_events.quote: This is not a coaching sim. The WVL is an ownership sim — you make high-level decisions and watch the consequences play out through layers of delegation.

delegation_events.decision: Decoupled from college dynasty — completely independent modes with optional JSON bridge
delegation_events.quote: The two modes are completely independent. No shared state, no code coupling. The bridge is a JSON file on disk.

actions.description: New engine/wvl_config.py: 64 clubs, 4 tier configs, 13 rivalry groups
New engine/promotion_relegation.py: pro/rel engine with custom rules per boundary, playoff simulation
New engine/wvl_free_agency.py: FA pool (import or synthetic), AI signing, owner targeted FA
New engine/wvl_owner.py: 7 owner archetypes, 6 president archetypes, investment system, financials
New engine/wvl_dynasty.py: multi-season dynasty with offseason pipeline, save/load
New scripts/generate_wvl_teams.py: 64-team generation with country-appropriate names
engine/player_card.py: 5 optional pro fields (age, pro_team, contract_years, contract_salary, pro_status)
nicegui_app/app.py: added WVL to NAV_SECTIONS + routing
Generated data: 64 club JSON files in data/wvl_teams/ + data/wvl_tier_assignments.json

risk_posture: All new player card fields are Optional with None defaults — no impact on existing college code

validation_claims.claim: 34/34 tests passing across TestWVLConfig, TestOwnerMode, TestFreeAgency, TestProDevelopment, TestPromotionRelegation, TestWVLDynasty, TestPlayerCardProFields, TestTeamData.
No regressions in existing test suite (pre-existing failure in test_recruiting.py::test_roster_prestige_estimation is unrelated — verified failing on clean branch before changes).
validation_claims.check_method: Run full test suite and compare failure set to pre-change baseline

non_actions.item: Full season simulation through the UI — week-by-week stepping with standings display is stubbed
non_actions.reason: multi-tier sim orchestrator works in engine but UI only has basic sim button

non_actions.item: Offseason UI flow — investment allocation, president hire/fire, targeted FA selection are engine-complete but need dedicated UI screens
College export UI button — export_graduating_class() wired but dynasty UI doesn't expose it yet
AI president coaching changes — coaching_style_bias exists but doesn't propagate to team's style dict between seasons

open_hooks: export_graduating_class() method wired and ready for UI exposure
Optional CVL graduate import via JSON bridge

3. One Targeted Free Agent per offseason
Generated Data (64 team files + 1 assignment file)
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/add-womens-pro-league-PIkzg. Commit: c412a86. Date: March 1, 2026. Narrative arcs: Wrexham (vanity project), Portland Timbers (lone American outpost), Vimpelin Veto (Tier 4 Cinderella), Nottingham Forest/Leeds/Everton (fallen giants), Bodø/Glimt. Owner archetype table: Sugar Daddy ($80M, 2 seasons patience, +15% FA rep), Patient Builder ($50M, 5 seasons), Youth Evangelist ($45M, 4 seasons), Trophy Hunter ($70M, 2 seasons), Hometown Hero ($40M, 4 seasons), Corporate Group ($65M, 3 seasons), Underdog Dreamer ($25M, 6 seasons). Investment allocation: Training Facilities (physical stats), Coaching Staff (mental stats), Stadium (FA attractiveness), Youth Academy (dev <25), Sports Science (stamina/power), Marketing/Brand (attractiveness/prestige). Player lifecycle differences table. 13 rivalry groups including El Clásico, English Triangle, Derby della Madonnina, North London Derby, Old Firm, etc.
parser_notes: Document titled 'Feature Report' not AAR — genre_confidence medium. Date confirmed: March 1, 2026.
